title: juniperus horizontalis f. alpina
intro: Juniperus horizontalis f. alpina, also known as Creeping Juniper or Creeping Mountain Juniper, is a low-growing, spreading evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with its essential oils and extracts exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Creeping Juniper include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health concerns. Creeping Juniper can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications such as salves and ointments for skin and wound care.
